response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced full-stack engineer with solid craftsmanship practices, but the profile does not match this role's core requirements. C++ experience is limited to a short stint at National Instruments and a thesis project, far from the required 8 years. Large-scale infrastructure exposure is real but brief (roughly two years of low-latency distributed systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, in DLang rather than C++), and there is no formal technical-leadership or cloud-platform ownership experience. Most of the career centers on web and product development rather than compute platforms.
strengths: Hands-on low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems experience (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus active mentoring via workshops and courses
Broad polyglot experience and long-running open-source project ownership (GISCollective, D testing tools)
concerns: Falls well short of the 8-year C++ requirement; primary systems language is DLang, not C++
No formal technical-lead role managing priorities, deadlines, and cross-team alignment
Limited production cloud architecture and compute-platform experience; career weighted toward web/full-stack product work

key_factors.factor: Lack of formal technical leadership experience
